methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The current supply of a Cred must exactly equal the sum of all shares held by all curators for that Cred.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the tracked supply is out of sync with actual distributed shares, the bonding curve price will be calculated incorrectly, allowing attackers to buy/sell at manipulated prices and drain the contract's ETH.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_credSupplyEqualsShareBalances(uint256 credId) public view { uint256 totalShares = 0; for(uint i=0; i<shareBalance[credId].length(); i++) { (, uint256 amount) = shareBalance[credId].at(i); totalShares += amount; } assert(creds[credId].currentSupply == totalShares); }

invariants.description: The total ETH balance of the RewardControl contract must be greater than or equal to the sum of all users' balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract's ETH balance falls below the sum of user balances, the protocol becomes insolvent and users will be unable to withdraw their earned rewards.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_rewardSolvency() public view { uint256 totalBalances = 0; /* sum over all users */ assert(address(this).balance >= totalBalances); }

invariants.description: The total ETH balance of the CuratorRewardsDistributor contract must be greater than or equal to the sum of all undistributed cred balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance is less than the sum of cred balances, the contract is insolvent, and curators will not be able to receive their distributed rewards during the distribute() call.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_curatorRewardsSolvency() public view { uint256 totalCredBalances = 0; /* sum over all credIds */ assert(address(this).balance >= totalCredBalances); }

invariants.description: The total mint fee paid by a user must exactly equal the sum of the protocol fee and the computed mint rewards (artist, referral, verifier, curate, and base mint fee).
invariants.attack_scenario: If the required ETH to mint is less than the distributed rewards, the protocol will become insolvent over time as it pays out more than it takes in.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_mintFeeConservation(uint256 artId, uint256 quantity) public view { assert(getArtMintFee(artId, quantity) == quantity * mintProtocolFee + IPhiRewards(phiRewardsAddress).computeMintReward(quantity, arts[artId].mintFee)); }

invariants.description: The number of minted tokens for any art must never exceed its configured maximum supply.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can mint more than maxSupply, it dilutes the value of the NFT, violates the creator's scarcity guarantee, and extracts unauthorized rewards.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_maxSupplyEnforced(uint256 artId) public view { assert(arts[artId].numberMinted <= arts[artId].maxSupply); }

invariants.description: A curator cannot sell shares if the current timestamp is less than or equal to their last trade timestamp plus the share lock period.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the lock period is bypassed, an attacker could perform flash-loan or atomic sandwich attacks on the bonding curve to extract MEV from other users' trades.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_shareLockPeriod(uint256 credId, address curator) public view { /* verified during state transitions in _handleTrade */ }

invariants.description: The buy price for a given amount of shares must strictly increase as the supply increases.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the price decreases or stays flat as supply increases, an attacker could buy shares and immediately sell them for a profit, draining the bonding curve's liquidity.
invariants.invariant_logic: getBuyPrice(supply + x, amount) > getBuyPrice(supply, amount) /* for x > 0 */
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_priceMonotonicity(uint256 supply, uint256 amount, uint256 x) public view { if(x > 0) assert(getBuyPrice(supply + x, amount) > getBuyPrice(supply, amount)); }
